task.json metadata field; verified against verifier.py line 36 and task description
setup_task.sh lines 53-61 loads first 10 spam_*.eml files to inbox
setup_task.sh lines 66-78 loads spam files 11+ (num_int > 10) into Junk folder, breaking at JUNK_LOADED >= 10
setup_task.sh lines 38-49: HAM_LOADED loop breaks at >= 40
README.md lines 76-80 documents source; verified via WebFetch to https://spamassassin.apache.org/old/publiccorpus/readme.html confirming real email data from public sources
SpamAssassin corpus documentation confirms emails are real messages from public fora, newsletters, and public sources; corpus contains 6047 messages of actual spam/ham with ~31% spam ratio

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims verified against setup scripts and verifier logic. The task uses the Apache SpamAssassin Public Corpus, a real dataset of 6,047 actual emails from public sources. Task plants exactly 10 real spam emails in inbox mixed with 40 real ham emails; initial Junk folder contains 10 pre-existing spam emails as baseline (metadata correctly specifies these counts: spam_planted_count=10, initial_junk_count=10). Report recipient is security-response@company.com. Custom folder name required is 'Spam-Incidents'. All data is real, not synthetic, sourced from the Apache SpamAssassin project's publicly available corpus.
